Workflow parse-safety — the 3 killers

Why Workflow scripts parse-fail "all the time": the script is a plain-JS string, and prompts are full of the chars JS uses to delimit/interpolate strings. Flip each snippet between the broken and fixed form — the panel runs your browser's real JS parser on it.

The rule that kills all three: inside any agent()/log() string — no backticks, no ${...}, no TS. Multi-line prompts: agent(['a','b'].join('\n')). When it keeps failing, stop re-sending the blob — every run persists the script to a file; edit the .js and re-run with Workflow({scriptPath}).
